assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can run Python and pip locally.
No GroqCloud account, organization, project, API key, or chat-completion request exists yet.

developer_goal: Create a GroqCloud account and API key, then run the official Python first chat completion.

first_success.outcome: The published Python chat-completion program returns and prints model message content.

route_selection.surface: GroqCloud console self-service web flow plus official Python Quickstart
route_selection.rule: Use the documented console API-key path and Python SDK first completion; do not substitute third-party SDKs.

candidates.name: Console signup or login, create API key, Python groq SDK chat completion
candidates.reason: Post-login key creation is documented at a high level, but account-creation fields and identity providers are not enumerable from the public login shell.

candidates.name: Same account path using curl, JavaScript, or third-party AI SDK examples on the Quickstart
candidates.reason: These reach a similar completion outcome on alternate client surfaces after the same unresolved account gate.

required_path.action: Sign up for or sign in to GroqCloud and accept the Services Agreement acknowledgement shown on the login shell.

required_fields.label: Services Agreement and Privacy Policy acknowledgement
Account identity and signup controls
required_fields.notes: Public login page states continuing accepts terms but does not expose a static email/OAuth field contract in fetched HTML.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has an authenticated GroqCloud console session.

required_path.action: Create an API key in the GroqCloud console and copy it.

required_fields.label: API key name or create-key form fields
required_fields.notes: Quickstart links to key creation; authenticated form fields are not public.

required_path.observable_result: An API key value is available to export as GROQ_API_KEY.

required_path.action: Export GROQ_API_KEY and install the Groq Python library.
required_path.observable_result: The groq package is installed and the key is present in the shell environment.

required_path.action: Run the published chat.completions.create example and print the message content.
required_path.observable_result: The terminal prints the chat-completion content.

prerequisites.description: Local Python and pip are available for the official Quickstart library install.

external_gates.description: Continuing on the login shell accepts the Groq Services Agreement and acknowledges the Privacy Policy.

platform_outcomes.description: Groq returns a chat completion for the submitted messages and model.

excluded.reason: Documented as a next step after the first completion, not the selected terminal.

excluded.item: Vercel AI SDK, LiteLLM, and LangChain examples
excluded.reason: Alternate client surfaces after the same account and key prerequisites.

sources.title: API Keys
evidence_supported: Authenticated API Keys console destination referenced by Quickstart

sources.title: GroqCloud login
evidence_supported: Services Agreement and Privacy Policy acknowledgement on continue

uncertainties.question: What identity providers, email fields, verification steps, and organization or project creation screens appear on a cold GroqCloud signup?
uncertainties.impact: Account-creation actions and fields cannot be reconstructed without inventing auth UI behind the login shell.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account walkthrough from console.groq.com/login through first authenticated dashboard and API key creation, including every required field.

uncertainties.question: What exact create-key form fields and secret-display rules appear on console.groq.com/keys for a new account?
uncertainties.impact: The credential step field count remains unverified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Authenticated screenshots or official docs that enumerate the current create-key controls.
